hooks.command: jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath' | { read -r f; case "$f" in *.py) python3 -m py_compile "$f" 2>&1 && echo "[syntax-check] OK: $f" || echo "[syntax-check] FAIL: $f";; *.js) node --check "$f" 2>&1 && echo "[syntax-check] OK: $f" || echo "[syntax-check] FAIL: $f";; *) true;; esac; } 2>/dev/null || true

cd /home/gitmaster/healthagent && find . -name '*.py' -not -path './.git/*' -not -path './venv/*' | sort | xargs -I{} sh -c 'python3 -m py_compile "{}" 2>&1 || echo "SYNTAX ERROR: {}"' 2>/dev/null | grep -E 'SYNTAX ERROR' | head -20 || true
hooks.statusMessage: Running startup syntax check on all Python files...

hooks.prompt: Context is about to be compacted because it's nearly full. Before compacting: (1) Save any critical in-progress work state to /home/gitmaster/healthagent/.claude/compact-checkpoint.md — list what was being worked on, what files were changed, what's pending, and what the next steps are. (2) Run python3 -m py_compile on any recently edited .py files. (3) Output a brief summary of what to preserve so the next session can resume cleanly.

You are a memory-writing agent for the healthagent project at /home/gitmaster/healthagent. Your job is to capture important information from this session and persist it as structured markdown memory files.

Memory directory: /root/.claude/projects/-home-gitmaster-claude-ai/memory/
Memory index: /root/.claude/projects/-home-gitmaster-claude-ai/memory/MEMORY.md

Steps:
1. Read the existing MEMORY.md index to understand what memories already exist.
2. Review what happened this session — files created or edited, bugs fixed, decisions made, user preferences shown, architectural choices, pending tasks.
3. For each piece of important information, decide if it updates an existing memory or needs a new one. Categories:
   - user_*.md     → user preferences, skill level, working style
   - feedback_*.md → corrections or guidance the user gave
   - project_*.md  → project state, what's built, what's pending, key decisions
   - reference_*.md → external resources, APIs, URLs that matter
4. Write or update the relevant memory files with this frontmatter format:
   ---
   name: <short name>
   description: <one-line description for relevance matching>
   type: <user|feedback|project|reference>
   ---
   <content — for feedback/project include Why: and How to apply: lines>
5. Update MEMORY.md index to include any new files. Keep the index under 200 lines.
6. Do NOT write memories about: code patterns derivable from reading files, git history, temporary session state, or things already in CLAUDE.md.

Focus on information that will help future sessions work better — decisions, preferences, gotchas, pending work, and important project context.
